facts.text: Seville, Florida was founded in 1882 by William Lente, a vice president of the Jacksonville, Tampa and Key West Railway, who chose the railroad right of way for the new town.
Shortly after its founding, Lente established the Bank of Seville and constructed a large brick residence that still stands as a reminder of the town’s early prosperity.
Lente teamed up with northern investors to build a grand hotel, marking Seville’s ambition to become a regional hospitality hub in the late 19th century.
The community invested $60,000 in a complex water system, one of the largest infrastructure projects in Volusia County at the time.
In 1889, tensions with his backers culminated in Lente’s tragic suicide at his Seville home, a dark chapter in the town’s early history.
Today, Seville remains a quiet, largely rural community just north of Pierson, reflecting its modest commercial development and agricultural roots.
